name: fastapi
summary: FastAPI framework, high performance, easy to learn, fast to code, ready for production

story.problem: Building Python APIs with Flask meant adding separate libraries for validation, serialization, and auto-generated docs — and none of them talked to each other. Django REST Framework was powerful but heavy. FastAPI was designed around the insight that Python type hints contain everything needed to handle all three automatically.
story.mental_model: In FastAPI, your function signatures are the API contract. Annotate a parameter as int and FastAPI validates the incoming value, rejects bad requests, and documents the type in the OpenAPI spec — all from the same annotation. Pydantic models define request/response shapes and handle serialization. The result is an API where the code and the docs are always in sync.
story.when_to_use: Use FastAPI when building REST or GraphQL APIs in Python, especially when you need async support, automatic OpenAPI/Swagger docs, request validation, or are building microservices. It's the best choice for new Python API projects.
story.when_not_to_use: FastAPI is API-only — it has no templating, admin interface, or ORM. For full-stack web apps with server-rendered HTML, Django is better. For tiny internal tools, Flask's smaller learning curve might be preferable.

alternatives.name: django
alternatives.reason: Full-featured framework with built-in ORM, admin, auth — better for full-stack apps

alternatives.name: flask
alternatives.reason: Simpler and more flexible; good for small APIs or when you want to choose every component yourself

alternatives.name: litestar
alternatives.reason: Similar philosophy to FastAPI but with different design choices; worth evaluating for new projects

nodes.label: fastapi
nodes.summary: Top-level module. Import FastAPI, APIRouter, Depends, etc.

nodes.label: FastAPI
nodes.summary: Main application class. Create with `app = FastAPI()`. Mount routers, middleware, lifecycle hooks.

nodes.label: APIRouter
nodes.summary: Group related endpoints into a router, then include in the app with app.include_router().

nodes.label: Path Operations (@app.get, @app.post…)
nodes.summary: Decorators that register route handlers: get, post, put, delete, patch.

nodes.label: Depends()
nodes.summary: Dependency injection. Pass functions as deps — FastAPI calls them and injects their return value.

nodes.label: Body / Query / Path / Header
nodes.summary: Declare request parameters with metadata, validation, and aliases.

nodes.label: Pydantic BaseModel (via fastapi)
nodes.summary: Request/response schemas. FastAPI auto-validates, parses, and documents them.

nodes.label: HTTPException
nodes.summary: Raise HTTP errors: `raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail='Not found')`.

nodes.label: BackgroundTasks
nodes.summary: Run tasks after returning response (email, logging) without blocking.

nodes.label: Middleware (@app.middleware)
nodes.summary: Intercept requests/responses globally. Used for CORS, auth, logging, timing.

nodes.label: Lifespan (startup/shutdown)
nodes.summary: async context manager for startup/shutdown logic (DB pool, ML model load).

nodes.label: Auto OpenAPI / Swagger UI
nodes.summary: FastAPI generates /docs (Swagger) and /redoc automatically from type hints.
